questions.question: In the StoryBrand framework, who is the hero of the story?

options.text: Your company (The Brand)
The Founder (You)
The Customer
The Marketplace

questions.explanation: One of the most critical shifts in StoryBrand is making the customer the hero, not your company. You are the Yoda (Guide), and the customer is the Luke Skywalker (Hero).

questions.question: What are the two components required to be a successful 'Guide' in messaging?

options.text: Innovation and Speed
Empathy and Authority
Low Price and High Value
Experience and Features

questions.explanation: A guide needs Empathy (demonstrating that you understand their pain) and Authority (demonstrating that you have the competence to solve it).

questions.question: What is an 'Internal Problem' in the StoryBrand model?

options.text: A bug in your internal codebase
How the external problem makes the customer feel (e.g., frustrated, worried)
A problem with your company's internal culture
A philosophical disagreement with competitors

questions.explanation: While the external problem is the surface-level issue, the internal problem is how it makes them feel. People buy solutions to internal problems more often than external ones.

questions.question: Why is 'The Plan' (3-4 simple steps) so important in messaging?

options.text: It proves that you have a complex methodology
It removes confusion and makes the 'buying journey' feel safe
It helps you organize your internal team
It allows you to justify a higher price point

questions.explanation: The moment a prospect feels confused, they stop buying. A simple 3-step plan removes cognitive load and tells the customer exactly what happens next, making them feel safe to proceed.

questions.question: What happens if a story (or your messaging) has no 'Stakes'?

options.text: It becomes a professional whitepaper
It becomes easier for everyone to understand
It becomes ignorable and fails to drive action
It attracts enterprise buyers who manage risk

questions.explanation: Without stakes (Success vs. Failure), there is no reason for the hero to act. Messaging without stakes is boring and fails to create the urgency needed for a sale.

questions.question: Draft a StoryBrand 'One-Liner' for your business following the 'Problem + Solution + Result' formula. Keep it under 30 words and focus on an internal pain point.
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate the student's StoryBrand one-liner. Check if it identifies: (1) a clear problem/pain, (2) the solution/guide's approach, (3) a specific result/transformation. Provide feedback on clarity and word economy.
